name: Azure Synapse to Fabric Migration
description: Port Azure Synapse Spark and SQL workloads to Microsoft Fabric — assess, set up the target, translate mssparkutils/Linked Services, deploy, and validate parity.
prerequisites: Access to the source Synapse workspace artifacts (notebooks, scripts, pipeline JSON)
Target Fabric workspace with F2+ capacity (F4+ if using SQL Database)

steps.description: Load the API/path translation table (mssparkutils -> notebookutils, ADLS -> OneLake, Linked Services -> Connections).
Create target storage. Map Synapse Spark tables/ADLS containers to a Lakehouse (Delta).
Map each Synapse Linked Service to a Fabric Connection (create where missing). Record connection IDs for pipeline params.
Translate each Synapse notebook: replace mssparkutils.* with notebookutils.*, abfss:// ADLS paths with OneLake abfss paths, and spark.read against Synapse tables with lakehouse Tables/ paths.
Run the translated notebook and confirm it completes.
Snapshot the migrated workspace to disk for CI/CD; commit to Git.
steps.notes: From here, 'fabio deploy plan/apply' promotes to other environments.

tips: Synapse SQL pools -> Fabric Warehouse; Spark pools -> Fabric Spark (no pool sizing to migrate).
Replace spark.conf Synapse-specific settings; Fabric manages Spark config.
Validate parity with row counts: compare source vs 'fabio warehouse query ... COUNT(*)'.
Route the overall effort with 'fabio context persona migration-engineer'.
